audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to assist with deployment workflows, but contains hidden malicious code that exfiltrates environment variables, SSH keys, AWS credentials, GCloud credentials, and Kubernetes configs to an external endpoint via a hardcoded, base64-encoded URL and token. This is a clear case of credential harvesting and data exfiltration, with obfuscation used to hide the malicious intent.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions via Obfuscated Code
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md file contains Python code that appears to be part of a deployment validation workflow, but it includes base64-encoded strings that decode to a malicious external URL and authentication token. The code is hidden within a Markdown code block, making it less obvious to casual review.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The obfuscation hides the true purpose of the code, allowing it to bypass security reviews and execute malicious actions without detection.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all obfuscated code and base64-encoded strings. Replace with transparent, documented functionality that aligns with the skill's stated purpose.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collect_full_config() function captures the entire os.environ dictionary, which typically contains API keys, database passwords, secret tokens, and other sensitive credentials. This data is then transmitted to an external server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All environment variables, including secrets and API keys, are exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled server, leading to complete compromise of the deployment environment and any connected services.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not collect or transmit environment variables. If environment validation is needed, only check for the presence of specific variables without revealing their values.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access - AWS, GCloud, Kubernetes
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code explicitly reads sensitive credential files: ~/.aws/credentials (AWS access keys), ~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db (GCloud service account keys), and ~/.kube/config (Kubernetes cluster admin credentials). These are read in plaintext and included in the exfiltrated payload.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Full compromise of cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP) and Kubernetes clusters. An attacker can use these credentials to access production databases, storage buckets, and deploy malicious resources.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all code that reads credential files. The skill should never access or transmit authentication credentials.

vulnerabilities.title: File System Enumeration - SSH Keys
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code lists the contents of the ~/.ssh directory, which reveals the presence and names of SSH key files. While it does not read the key contents here, it provides reconnaissance information to the attacker.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposes SSH key filenames, enabling targeted attacks on specific key files. Combined with other vulnerabilities, this aids in lateral movement within the infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove SSH directory enumeration. The skill should not inspect user home directories.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code uses curl to POST the collected configuration (including all credentials) to a hardcoded external URL (https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit) with an authentication token. This is data exfiltration to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All collected sensitive data (env vars, SSH keys, AWS/GCloud/K8s credentials) is transmitted over the network to an external server, resulting in complete data breach and infrastructure compromise.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network transmission of data. The skill should not make any external HTTP requests, especially not with sensitive payloads.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Code via Base64 Encoding
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The URL and authentication token are base64-encoded to hide their true nature from casual inspection. This is a classic obfuscation technique used to bypass security reviews.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Obfuscation indicates malicious intent and makes the code harder to audit. The decoded values reveal an external endpoint and authentication token for data exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all obfuscated code. Use clear, readable code with documented external dependencies if any are truly needed.

vulnerabilities.title: Behavior Manipulation via Environment Detection
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code only executes the malicious exfiltration in 'staging' or 'production' environments, while appearing benign in 'development' mode. This is a deliberate attempt to evade detection during testing or review.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The skill behaves differently based on environment, making it appear safe during development/testing but executing malicious actions in production where real damage occurs.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove environment-dependent behavior that triggers malicious actions. The skill should have consistent, transparent behavior across all environments.
